message:
🌱 backstage: [RFC] TechDocs Addons Framework (#1037)

* 🌱 Add backstage: [RFC] TechDocs Addons Framework mission

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* 🐛 Fix backstage-9636: replace wrong PR link and update resolution

PR #9561 (widen content pane) was unrelated to the TechDocs Addons
Framework RFC. Replace with the actual implementing PR #10203
([TechDocs] Initial Addon Framework Implementation, merged Mar 2022).
Update resolution summary to accurately describe the RFC outcome.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

+    "title": "backstage: [RFC] TechDocs Addons Framework",
+    "description": "[RFC] TechDocs Addons Framework. Requested by 33+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current backstage setup",
+        "description": "Verify your backstage version and configuration:\n```bash\nnpx backstage-cli info\n```\nThis feature requires a working backstage installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review backstage configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant backstage configuration:\n**Status:** Open for comments\n\n## Need\n\nTechDocs is a centralized platform for publishing, viewing, and discovering technical documentation across an entire organization."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for [RFC] TechDocs Addons Framework",
+        "description": "### Configuration matrix\n\nDepending on each addon location and addon scope, there is a need to configure each combination. The table below categorizes the intended options given to both app integrators and documentation creators. \n\n|                                                           | `GLOBAL` scope     | `SITE` scope     | `PAGE` scope     |\n| ----------------------------------- | -------------------- | ---------------- | ----------------- |\n| Virtual location `COMPONENT`   |  <sub>(not applicable)<sup>5</sup></sub>  | <sub>(not applicable)<sup>5</sup></sub>  | Register in scope<sup>2</sup> + Use addon tag in Markdown content<sup>4</sup>  |\n| Virtual location `CONTENT`         | Register in routes<sup>1</sup>     | Register in scope<sup>2</sup> + register in `mkdocs.yml`<sup>3</sup>  | Register in scope<sup>2</sup> + register in `mkdocs.yml`<sup>3</sup> + Use addon tag in Markdown content<sup>4</sup>  |\n\nThe RFC was implemented in PR #10203 ([TechDocs] Initial Addon Framework Implementation), merged March 2022."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected.\nConfirm the feature described in \"[RFC] TechDocs Addons Framework\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "The TechDocs Addons Framework RFC (issue #9636) was a proposal to extend TechDocs with a pluggable addon system. The RFC was accepted and implemented in PR #10203 ([TechDocs] Initial Addon Framework Implementation), merged March 2022. The framework allows integrators to register addons at various scopes (GLOBAL, SITE, PAGE) and virtual locations (COMPONENT, CONTENT).",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "### Configuration matrix\n\nDepending on each addon location and addon scope, there is a need to configure each combination. The table below categorizes the intended options given to both app integrators and documentation creators. \n\n|                                                           | `GLOBAL` scope     | `SITE` scope     | `PAGE` scope     |\n| ----------------------------------- | -------------------- | ---------------- | ----------------- |\n| Virtual location `COMPONENT`   |  <sub>(not applicable)<sup>5</sup></sub>  | <sub>(not applicable)<sup>5</sup></sub>  | Register in scope<sup>2</sup> + Use addon tag in Markdown content<sup>4</sup>  |\n| Virtual location `CONTENT`         | Register in routes<sup>1</sup>     | Register in scope<sup>2</sup> + register in `mkdocs.yml`<sup>3</su",
+        "#### 2) Register in scope",
+        "#### 3) Register in `mkdocs.yml`"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
